creative_brief: Split-screen visual: left side shows a long, winding conveyor belt with six generic toll-booth style checkpoints between a farm and a kitchen table. Right side shows a single clean line from farm to a FarmThru hub. Muted, documentary tone. No text overlay beyond the FarmThru logo.
primary_text:
180km. That's how far Rachel's beef travels from Kempsey to families in Sydney.

Supermarket beef? Closer to 2,400km through warehouses and wholesalers.

We built FarmThru to close that gap. Farm-direct grocery, no unnecessary middlemen. Around 15 to 20 partner farms across NSW, all traceable. Order online, collect from Brookvale Monday to Friday.

No subscription. No lock-in. Just food you can trace back to the person who grew it.

We're building something worth owning. You can be part of it.
headline: 180km, not 2,400. Own a piece of why.
description: Farm-direct grocery changing how Sydney families eat. No middlemen, no lock-in.
